creative.concept: v1.15 release day — 'The pill is yours now.' Release card with 6-feature chip grid; red accent on Movable pill; decorative 'move me' pill outline top-right reinforces the drag gesture. No carousel (ran yesterday). Storyboard shows the drag motion for the first time.

cards.title: v1.15 — The pill is yours now.
cards.use: Primary visual for LinkedIn, X, Instagram, and Show HN today. Export as 1080×1080 PNG. The red 'Movable pill' chip against dark background is the scroll-stopper; the 'move me' pill outline top-right hints at the drag interaction.

storyboard.title: The movable pill — drag, position, dictate
storyboard.use: Instagram Reel / X video / GIF — first clip ever showing the pill being repositioned; the drag gesture is inherently visual and earns a second take from anyone who has used Jot before

shots.visual: Mac desktop, Dynamic Island-style pill sitting under the notch in its default position — established, familiar
Hand reaches up, grabs the pill, drags it smoothly to the bottom-left corner of the screen; pill follows the cursor with fluid motion
Hand presses ⌥ Space; pill in its new position begins pulsing with an audio waveform
User speaks; pill pulses in the corner; text appears at cursor in VS Code or Mail
Wide desktop: pill resting in corner, text visible in editor; jot-transcribe.com watermark lower-right

drafts.text:
v1.15 shipped at 5am.

The dictation pill is movable now — drag it anywhere on screen, corner, edge, wherever doesn't interrupt your layout. Previously it was anchored under the notch.

Also: better recordings search, cleaner transcripts, smarter vocabulary, simpler settings.

macOS 15 required from today. Still $0. Still on-device. Still nothing leaves your Mac.

Where would you put a floating status indicator?

#macOS #indiedev #buildinpublic

drafts.text:
Jot v1.15 — the dictation pill is movable now (free, open-source Mac dictation)

Quick background: I built Jot because I was paying $85/year for a Mac dictation app and decided the on-device transcription tech was commoditized enough that a free version should exist. Jot sits in the menu bar: press a hotkey, speak, transcript pastes wherever your cursor is in any app. Transcription runs entirely on the Apple Neural Engine — no cloud, no account, no audio leaves the Mac.

v1.15 shipped this morning. Here's what changed:

**Movable dictation pill.** The floating status indicator was previously anchored under the Mac notch. Now you can drag it anywhere on screen and it remembers the position. Most-requested feature, especially from people with external monitors or window layouts where the notch area is occupied.

**Better recordings search.** Fuzzy search across all stored transcripts, not just the current session. Useful for finding something you dictated last week.

**Smarter custom vocabulary.** The vocabulary booster improves from corrections over time. Product names and proper nouns that keep mistranscribing get smarter after a few manual fixes.

**Cleaner transcripts.** Post-processing pass improved; fewer hallucinated filler words.

**Simpler settings.** Tighter layout, less visual noise.

**More languages (experimental).** Expanding the supported language set beyond the current roster — still in progress.

**macOS 15 now required.** I raised the deployment floor from macOS 14 to macOS 15 to access CoreMLLLM for on-device AI. If you're on Sonoma (macOS 14), the last compatible release is v1.14.1.

Apple Silicon only. Completely free. Open source on GitHub.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments.

drafts.text: Show HN: Jot v1.15 – free on-device Mac dictation; pill is draggable, better search
drafts.note: Post TODAY — it's Tuesday, the ideal HN window. The utm_url goes in the submission URL field (not the text). First comment: 'Hi HN! Jot is a free, open-source Mac dictation app I've been building since April. Press a hotkey (⌥Space by default), speak, and the transcript pastes wherever your cursor is — terminal, Mail, VS Code, Slack, any text field. Transcription runs entirely on-device on the Apple Neural Engine (NVIDIA Parakeet TDT). No cloud, no account, no audio leaves the Mac. v1.15 ships two most-requested changes: (1) The dictation pill is now draggable — previously pinned under the notch; you can drag it anywhere and it remembers the position. External-monitor users have been asking for this since v1.0. (2) Better recordings search — fuzzy search across all stored transcripts, not just the current session. Also: cleaner transcripts, simpler settings, smarter custom vocabulary, experimental more-language support. macOS floor bumped to 15 (was 14) to access CoreMLLLM for on-device AI features. Stack: SwiftUI + AppKit (NSPanel for the pill), FluidAudio wrapping Parakeet for inference, sindresorhus/KeyboardShortcuts for hotkeys, Sparkle for auto-update. Repo: https://github.com/vineetu/JOT-Transcribe' — Never solicit upvotes.

opportunities.title: 11 Best Superwhisper Alternatives in 2026 — Voibe
opportunities.why: High-SEO alternatives roundup surfacing on every 'superwhisper free alternative' search; Jot not listed despite being the only fully-free open-source on-device option. Voibe allows reader comments; the v1.15 release is a natural hook for a first-time mention.
opportunities.suggested_reply: Great list. One worth adding: Jot (jot-transcribe.com) — free, open-source, fully on-device on the Apple Neural Engine. No subscription, no account, no audio leaves the Mac. Hotkey, speak, text at cursor. Just shipped v1.15 with a draggable pill and better search. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.)

opportunities.title: SuperWhisper Alternative 2026: Free Local + BYOK Cloud — Spokenly
opportunities.why: Competitor comparison page — readers actively choosing are exactly Jot's audience. Not comment-enabled; flag for outreach to spokenly.app blog on June 26.

notes: v1.15 shipped at 05:01 UTC today (Tue Jun 23) — first release requiring macOS 15. Headline feature is the movable dictation pill, most-requested change since launch. Total 206 (+12 from Jun 22's 194): v1.14.1 moved 75→84, v1.15 at 3 within ~6h of release; v1.13.1=28, v1.13=12, v1.12=2, v1.11=11, v1.10=2 confirmed; older releases estimated at 64 from Jun 22 baseline. avg7=11/day — accelerating. Show HN eligible today (Tue 6–8am ET). All four channels recommended:true (Reddit ≥4 days since Jun 19; Instagram ≥3 days since Jun 20). iTunes API 403 (9th day); appstore.json date updated, all values preserved. Outreach window reopens June 26 — priority: machow2.com, speakmac.app, and a direct Voibe comment on the superwhisper-alternatives page. The movable pill is Jot's best visual marketing moment to date — recording the 10–15s drag Reel today while the release is fresh is the highest-leverage action of the week.
